prompt_text: Open source or affordable MLOps tools instead of Comet
response:
# Open Source & Affordable MLOps Alternatives to Comet

## **Completely Free & Open Source**

### **MLflow**
- Experiment tracking, model registry, deployment
- Industry standard, widely adopted
- Self-hosted option
- [mlflow.org](https://mlflow.org)

### **Weights & Biases (W&B)**
- Free tier for open source/personal projects
- Excellent UI and collaboration features
- [wandb.ai](https://wandb.ai)

### **Neptune.ai**
- Generous free tier (500GB/month)
- Good for small teams
- [neptune.ai](https://neptune.ai)

### **DVC (Data Version Control)**
- Open source, Git-like for ML
- Experiment tracking + data versioning
- [dvc.org](https://dvc.org)

### **Hydra** (Meta)
- Configuration management
- Lightweight, focuses on experiment management
- [hydra.cc](https://hydra.cc)

---

## **Self-Hosted Options**

| Tool | Cost | Best For |
|------|------|----------|
| **MLflow** | Free (OSS) | General tracking, model registry |
| **Kubeflow** | Free (OSS) | Kubernetes workflows |
| **Airflow** | Free (OSS) | Pipeline orchestration |
| **JupyterHub** | Free (OSS) | Team notebooks |

---

## **Budget-Friendly Commercial**

- **Weights & Biases**: $0-400/month (free tier available)
- **Neptune**: $0-500/month (free tier generous)
- **Aim**: Free & open source
- **Guild AI**: Free community version

---

## **My Recommendation**
**Start with MLflow + W&B free tier** for the best balance of features and cost.

What's your primary use case (experiment tracking, model deployment, data versioning)?
